opinion.h: McLEMORE v. MISSISSIPPI. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF MISSISSIPPI. No. 410. Decided November 20, 1961.
opinion.p: W. E. Gore, Sr. for appellant.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
opinion.h: FRUHLING v. AMALGAMATED HOUSING CORP., 368 U.S. 70 (1961)
opinion.p: 368 U.S. 70 FRUHLING v. AMALGAMATED HOUSING CORP. ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 419. Decided November 20, 1961.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Louis J. Lefkowitz, Attorney General of New York, Paxton Blair, Solicitor General, and Daniel M. Cohen, Assistant Attorney General, for appellees.
The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
